title: lobelia gregoriana subsp. gregoriana
intro: Lobelia gregoriana subsp. gregoriana, also known as the Alpine Lobelia, is a rare and endangered plant species found in the mountainous regions of the Himalayas. This plant is a perennial herb that contains alkaloids, specifically lobeline, which has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The medicinal properties of Lobelia gregoriana subsp. gregoriana include its ability to act as a bronchodilator, making it a potential treatment for respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to have health benefits including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function.
